text.title: testing-django-pytest Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for testing-django-pytest. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Django tests MUST use pytest function style (def test_...) with @pytest.mark.django_db rather than unittest.TestCase subclasses. Object graphs MUST be built with factory_boy factories, not raw ORM creates or fixtures that rot with model changes.
rule.rationale: Function style composes with pytest fixtures without super() calls. factory_boy factories evolve with the model and produce readable, minimal object graphs. Raw ORM creates in tests produce fragile tests that break on field additions.

rule.statement: conftest.py MUST NOT use raw User.objects.create_user() calls. All object creation MUST go through factory_boy factories so tests survive model changes without fixture rewrites.
rule.rationale: Fixtures that call ORM directly accumulate required-field calls that break silently when the model gains non-nullable fields. factory_boy factories define sensible defaults and require only the fields relevant to the test.

rule.statement: Duplicate test functions that differ only in input/output MUST be collapsed into a single @pytest.mark.parametrize block. Test names MUST describe the concept being tested, not a sequence number (test_create_1, test_create_2 is forbidden).
rule.rationale: One assertion concept per test named after the concept. Sequential names make failures uninterpretable without reading the body.
